race.name: Rennsteig Ski-Lauf
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers along the historic Rennsteig ridgeway through the Thuringian Forest — the winter sibling of Germany's legendary Rennsteiglauf trail race.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations along the Rennsteig trail

climate.description: February in the Thuringian Forest at 500-980m means variable conditions. Temperatures range from -10°C to 2°C. Snow reliability is moderate — the Rennsteig ridgeway benefits from its elevation but climate change has reduced consistent cover in recent decades. Oberhof's biathlon infrastructure ensures excellent grooming when snow permits.
challenges: Variable snow conditions — low-mountain altitude makes coverage inconsistent
Wind exposure on the open Rennsteig ridgeway
Temperature fluctuations can create icy or slushy patches
Fog common in the Thuringian Forest

course.primary: A 30km course along the historic Rennsteig ridgeway, the longest continuously signposted long-distance ski trail in Central Europe at 142km total. The race section passes through the dense Thuringian Forest at 500-980m altitude, combining forested trail skiing with open ridge sections offering views across central Germany.
course.surface: Groomed trails along the Rennsteig ridgeway through mixed forest
course.grooming: Oberhof-standard grooming — benefits from Germany's biathlon center infrastructure
features: Historic Rennsteig ridgeway — 142km of signposted ski trail
Winter counterpart to the famous Rennsteiglauf running race
Central European accessibility — easy train and car connections

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Historic Rennsteig setting and Oberhof infrastructure boost grooming and accessibility. Snow reliability is the weakness — low-mountain altitude makes consistent cover uncertain. Primarily German/regional participation.

history.summary: The Rennsteig Ski-Lauf is the winter counterpart to the famous Rennsteiglauf trail running race, following the historic Rennsteig ridgeway through the Thuringian Forest. The Rennsteig is the longest continuously signposted long-distance ski trail in Central Europe at 142km. Oberhof, the race base, is Germany's premier winter sports center and home to the biathlon World Cup venue, providing excellent infrastructure for the event.
notable_facts: Winter counterpart to the famous Rennsteiglauf running race
Set along the Rennsteig — the longest signposted ski trail in Central Europe (142km)
Based in Oberhof, Germany's premier biathlon and winter sports center
Offers both classic and freestyle distances
The Thuringian Forest has centuries of skiing heritage

videos.title: Rennsteig Rollskilauf Gräfenroda - Oberhof 2023
videos.description: Am Sonntag den 1. Oktober 2023 übertragen wir ab 8:55 Uhr den Rennsteig Rollskilauf von Gräfenroda nach Oberhof mit internationalen sowie nationalen Stars der Skilanglauf-Szene.
videos.curation_reason: Recent race coverage with international stars on the Rennsteig course from Gräfenroda to Oberhof.

videos.title: Rennsteig  Rollskilauf Ankündigung 2019
videos.description: Der Rennsteig Rollskilauf führt am 29.September 2019 über 21,4 Kilometer von Gräfenroda nach Oberhof. Die Skilangläufer des DSV haben die Strecke schon getestet und werden auch beim Rennen am Start stehen. Und was ist mit dir?

videos.title: xc-ski.de Langlaufski- und Skirollertest 2012 in Oberhof
videos.description: http://www..xc-ski.de - Vom 14.-17. Juni testete das xc-ski.de-Team in Oberhof die aktuellsten Skiroller und die Langlaufbretter der kommenden Saison auf Herz und Nieren. Auf der Rollerstrecke rund um das Oberhofer Skistadion und in der Skisporthalle hatten an zwei Tagen auch interessierte Langlaufsportler die Chance, sich einen Überblick über das neue Material zu verschaffen. Hier ein kurzer Clip vom verlängerten Testwochenende in Oberhof ...

videos.title: Technique Analysis Of An Elite Skier | Double Poling
videos.description:
Youtube Chapters
00:00 - Intro
00:32 - Technique Is Individual
01:12 - Mobility and Cheetah-Jumps
02:19 - Frequency
02:55 - Forward Fall and Core Strength
03:40 - POWER ISN'T THE ONLY THING THAT MATTERS
04:56 - Heel Lift Or Not
06:55 - Arm Position
07:34 - More Mobility Isn't Always Better
08:22 - Straight Legs Are More Economical
09:04 - Pole Plant
09:47 - Hook-Like Head Position
10:12 - Master Of DP

hello guys in today's video we will do

is probably the best technician of those

before we start when talking about

like training volume there isn't one
single correct answer people are built
differently have different anatomies
moment arms and have difference in
strength and Mobility so just because a

most effective movement pattern for your
body there are a lot more factors

efficient than others but this video
isn't meant to dive deep into the topic
of cost of travel today we will simply
have a look at why geralin is so
excellent firstly Mobility this is a
fundamental quality required in order to
move technically efficient and is
neglected by the great majority of the
skiing Community if this is something he
has worked towards or if he just has
this Mobility naturally I can't say but
regardless of how he got flexible he has
obviously a huge Advantage there take a
look at his back movements in particular
when he is seeking maximum speed in
gentle downhill terrain he has a very
significant fall in the upper body the
speed is a product of the frequency and
the distance per stroke because his
upper body is not limited anywhere he is
able to almost jump forward almost like
a cheetah if your whole upper body and
especially your back has limited
Mobility it will limit your possibility
of achieving maximum stroke length and
double pulling on on flats and in
downhills in uphills having these long
jumps won't be practical so it won't
matter in that type of terrain but
imagine in flat terrain if you lose 10

race geelan has a relatively low

the hips he is able to have the chest
and hips forward on the ski you can
measure the frequency the frequency will
always vary depending on the degree of
incline but in general having over one
stroke per second or 60 Strokes per
minute will be classified as a very high
frequency and having less than 40
Strokes per minute will be seen as a
very low frequency he is one of the

secondly as you can see he has a quite

this isn't possible to do without
sufficient overall mobility and neither

strength he has obviously an extremely
strong
core this is something you can improve

strength exercises can help you control

to make sure you don't bend over too
much but are able to hold back
you obviously will have to bend over a

in this type of terrain when you're
really trying to go fast too much bowing
will not be
effective in downhill double polling
maximizing the length of the stroke is
the most important thing the distance

where the actual power output only will
be one of several determining factors
core strength mobility and Technical
abilities will also influence so just
because a guy is powerful for example on

to me as a guy who has less power than

but he is just able to coordinate his

the lack of
power you can see in pure uphill races
